faqs.question: Why are crickets so common in St. Albans homes?
faqs.answer: St. Albans' abundant vegetation, mature trees, and proximity to parks like Roy Wilkins Park and Baisley Pond Park provide ideal cricket habitats. Crickets are attracted to the moisture in basements and crawl spaces common in St. Albans homes, and they enter through small cracks in foundations, gaps under doors, and openings around utility lines. The neighborhood's residential density and landscaping create perfect conditions for cricket populations to establish themselves near homes.

faqs.question: What does your cricket control service include in St. Albans?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive cricket control service includes a thorough inspection of your St. Albans property to identify entry points and harborage areas, targeted treatment of baseboards, cracks, crevices, and exterior perimeter using professional-grade products, removal of existing crickets, sealing recommendations for entry points, and moisture control advice. We also provide follow-up service to ensure complete elimination and can schedule preventative treatments to keep your home cricket-free year-round.
